page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring that each of the 8 vCPUs maps directly to a dedicated physical execution unit without hyperthreading.

The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio aligns with standard general-purpose computing requirements. The instance does not include any bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation model ensures predictable performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. The absence of bundled local storage and GPU hardware minimizes baseline resource overhead, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that rely entirely on external storage volumes. This profile is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, the instance features 8 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.g9i.2xlarge offers a predictable, dedicated resource profile. It is designed for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require dedicated compute resources without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration.
